volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hecuba (Section 225 - Section 399)

sections.label: Section 225
sections.original: οἶσθʼ οὖν ὃ δρᾶσον ; μήτʼ ἀποσπασθῇς βίᾳ
sections.translation: Do you know then what to do ? Do not be forcibly torn from her , nor match your might against mine ; recognize the limits of your strength , and the presence of your troubles . Even in adversity it is wise to yield to reason ’ s dictates .

sections.label: Section 229
sections.original: αἰαῖ · παρέστηχʼ , ὡς ἔοικʼ , ἀγὼν μέγας ,
sections.translation: Alas ! a dreadful trial is near , it seems ,

sections.label: Section 230
sections.original: πλήρης στεναγμῶν οὐδὲ δακρύων κενός .
sections.translation: full of mourning , rich in tears . Yes , I too escaped death where death had been my due , and Zeus did not destroy me but is still preserving my life , that I may witness in my misery fresh sorrows surpassing all before . But if the bond may ask the free

sections.label: Section 235
sections.original: μὴ λυπρὰ μηδὲ καρδίας δηκτήρια
sections.translation: of things that do not grieve them or wrench their heart-strings , you ought to speak in answer to my questions and I ought to hear what you have to say .

sections.label: Section 238
sections.original: ἔξεστʼ , ἐρώτα · τοῦ χρόνου γὰρ οὐ φθονῶ .
sections.translation: Granted ; put your questions ; I do not grudge you that delay .

sections.label: Section 239
sections.original: οἶσθʼ ἡνίκʼ ἦλθες Ἰλίου κατάσκοπος ,
sections.translation: Do you know when you came to spy on Ilium ,

sections.label: Section 240
sections.original: δυσχλαινίᾳ τʼ ἄμορφος , ὀμμάτων τʼ ἄπο
sections.translation: disguised in rags and tatters , while down your cheek ran drops of blood ?

sections.label: Section 242
sections.original: οἶδʼ · οὐ γὰρ ἄκρας καρδίας ἔψαυσέ μου .
sections.translation: I do ; for it was no slight impression it made upon my heart .

sections.label: Section 243
sections.original: ἔγνω δέ σʼ Ἑλένη καὶ μόνῃ κατεῖπʼ ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: Did Helen recognize you and tell me only ?

sections.label: Section 244
sections.original: μεμνήμεθʼ ἐς κίνδυνον ἐλθόντες μέγαν .
sections.translation: I well remember the great risk I ran .

sections.label: Section 245
sections.original: ἥψω δὲ γονάτων τῶν ἐμῶν ταπεινὸς ὤν ;
sections.translation: Did you embrace my knees in all humility ?

sections.label: Section 246
sections.original: ὥστʼ ἐνθανεῖν γε σοῖς πέπλοισι χεῖρʼ ἐμήν .
sections.translation: Yes , so that my hand grew dead and cold upon your robe .

sections.label: Section 247
sections.original: ἔσωσα δῆτά σʼ ἐξέπεμψά τε χθονός ;
sections.translation: Was it I that saved and sent you forth again ?

sections.label: Section 248
sections.original: ὥστʼ εἰσορᾶν γε φέγγος ἡλίου τόδε .
sections.translation: You did , and so I still behold the light of day .

sections.label: Section 249
sections.original: τί δῆτʼ ἔλεξας δοῦλος ὢν ἐμὸς τότε ;
sections.translation: What did you say then , when in my power ?

sections.label: Section 250
sections.original: πολλῶν λόγων εὑρήμαθʼ , ὥστε μὴ θανεῖν .
sections.translation: Doubtless I found plenty to say , to save my life .

sections.label: Section 251
sections.original: οὔκουν κακύνῃ τοῖσδε τοῖς βουλεύμασιν ,
sections.translation: Are not you then playing a sorry part to plot against me thus , after the kind treatment you by your own confession received from me , showing me no gratitude but all the ill you can ? A thankless race ! all you who covet honor from the mob

sections.label: Section 255
sections.original: ζηλοῦτε τιμάς · μηδὲ γιγνώσκοισθέ μοι ,
sections.translation: for your oratory . Oh that you were unknown to me ! you who harm your friends and think no more of it , if you can say a word to win the mob . But tell me , what kind of cleverness did they think it , when against this child they passed their bloody vote ?

sections.label: Section 260
sections.original: πότερα τὸ χρῆν σφʼ ἐπήγαγʼ ἀνθρωποσφαγεῖν
sections.translation: Was it duty that led them to slay a human victim at the tomb , where sacrifice of oxen is more fitting ? or does Achilles , if claiming the lives of those who slew him as his recompense , show his justice by marking her out for death ? No ! she at least never injured him .

sections.label: Section 265
sections.original: Ἑλένην νιν αἰτεῖν χρῆν τάφῳ προσφάγματα ·
sections.translation: He should have demanded Helen as a victim at his tomb , for she it was that proved his ruin , bringing him to Troy ; or if some captive of surpassing beauty was to be singled out for death , this did not point to us ; for the daughter of Tyndareus was fairest of all ,

sections.label: Section 270
sections.original: ἀδικοῦσά θʼ ἡμῶν οὐδὲν ἧσσον ηὑρέθη .
sections.translation: and her injury to him was proved no less than ours . Against the justice of his plea I pit this argument . Now hear the recompense due from you to me at my request . On your own confession , you fell at my feet and embraced my hand and aged cheek ;

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: ἀνθάπτομαί σου τῶνδε τῶν αὐτῶν ἐγὼ
sections.translation: I in my turn now do the same to you , and claim the favor then bestowed ; and I implore you , do not tear my child from my arms or slay her ; there are dead enough . In her I take delight and forget my sorrows ;

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: ἥδʼ ἀντὶ πολλῶν ἐστί μοι παραψυχή ,
sections.translation: she is my comfort in place of many a loss , my city and my nurse , my staff and journey ’ s guide . It is not right that those in power should use it out of season , or , when prosperous , suppose they will be always so . For I also was prosperous once , but now my life is lived ,

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: τὸν πάντα δʼ ὄλβον ἦμαρ ἕν μʼ ἀφείλετο .
sections.translation: and one day robbed me of all my bliss . Friend , by your beard , have some regard and pity for me ; go to Achaea ’ s army , and talk them over , saying how hateful a thing it is to slay women whom at first you spared out of pity ,

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: βωμῶν ἀποσπάσαντες , ἀλλʼ ᾠκτίρατε .
sections.translation: after dragging them from the altars . For among you the same law holds good for slave and free alike respecting bloodshed ; such a reputation as yours will persuade them even though its words are weak ; for the same argument , when proceeding from those of no account ,

sections.label: Section 295
sections.original: κἀκ τῶν δοκούντων αὑτὸς οὐ ταὐτὸν σθένει .
sections.translation: has not the same force as when it is uttered by men of mark .

sections.label: Section 296
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν οὕτω στερρὸς ἀνθρώπου φύσις ,
sections.translation: Human nature is not so stony-hearted as to hear your plaintive tale and catalogue of sorrows , without shedding a tear .

sections.label: Section 299
sections.original: Ἑκάβη , διδάσκου , μηδὲ τῷ θυμουμένῳ
sections.translation: O Hecuba , be schooled by me , and do not in your angry mood

sections.label: Section 300
sections.original: τὸν εὖ λέγοντα δυσμενῆ ποιοῦ φρενός .
sections.translation: count him a foe who speaks wisely . Your life I am prepared to save , for the service I received ; I do not say otherwise . But what I said to all , I will not now deny , that after Troy ’ s capture I would give your daughter to the chief man of our army

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: σὴν παῖδα δοῦναι σφάγιον ἐξαιτουμένῳ .
sections.translation: because he asked a victim . For here is a source of weakness to many states , whenever a man of brave and generous soul receives no greater honor than his inferiors . Now Achilles , lady , deserves honor at our hands ,

sections.label: Section 310
sections.original: θανὼν ὑπὲρ γῆς Ἑλλάδος κάλλιστʼ ἀνήρ .
sections.translation: since on behalf of Hellas the man died most nobly . Is not this a foul reproach to treat him as a friend in life , but , when he is gone from us , to treat him so no more ? Enough ! what will they say , if once more there comes a gathering of the army and a contest with the foe ?

sections.label: Section 315
sections.original: πότερα μαχούμεθʼ ἢ φιλοψυχήσομεν ,
sections.translation: Shall we fight or nurse our lives , seeing the dead have no honors ? For myself , indeed , when alive , if my daily store were scant , yet it would be all-sufficient , but my tomb I should wish

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: τὸν ἐμὸν ὁρᾶσθαι · διὰ μακροῦ γὰρ ἡ χάρις .
sections.translation: to be an object of respect , for this gratitude has long to run . You speak of cruel sufferings ; hear my answer . Among us are grey old women and men no less miserable than you , and brides bereft of gallant husbands ,

sections.label: Section 325
sections.original: ὧν ἥδε κεύθει σώματʼ Ἰδαία κόνις .
sections.translation: over whom this Trojan dust has closed . Endure these sorrows ; for us , if we are wrong in resolving to honor the brave , we shall bring upon ourselves a charge of ignorance ; but as for you barbarians , do not regard your friends as such and pay no homage to your gallant dead ,

sections.label: Section 330
sections.original: θαυμάζεθʼ , ὡς ἂν ἡ μὲν Ἑλλὰς εὐτυχῇ ,
sections.translation: so that Hellas may prosper and you may reap the fruits of such policy .

sections.label: Section 332
sections.original: αἰαῖ · τὸ δοῦλον ὡς κακὸν πέφυκʼ ἀεὶ
sections.translation: Alas ! how cursed is slavery always in its nature , forced by the might of the stronger to endure unseemly treatment .

sections.label: Section 334
sections.original: ὦ θύγατερ , οὑμοὶ μὲν λόγοι πρὸς αἰθέρα
sections.translation: Daughter , my pleading to avert your bloody death

sections.label: Section 335
sections.original: φροῦδοι μάτην ῥιφέντες ἀμφὶ σοῦ φόνου ·
sections.translation: was wasted idly , hurled forth on the air ; but you , if endowed with greater power than your mother , make haste to utter every pleading note like the tuneful nightingale , to save your soul from death . Throw yourself pitiably at Odysseus ’ knees ,

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: καὶ πεῖθʼ — ἔχεις δὲ πρόφασιν · ἔστι γὰρ τέκνα
sections.translation: and try to move him — here is your plea : he too has children — to feel pity for your sad fate .

sections.label: Section 342
sections.original: ὁρῶ σʼ , Ὀδυσσεῦ , δεξιὰν ὑφʼ εἵματος
sections.translation: Odysseus , I see you hiding your right hand beneath your robe and turning away your face , so that I may not touch your beard .

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: θάρσει · πέφευγας τὸν ἐμὸν Ἱκέσιον Δία ·
sections.translation: Take heart ; you are safe from the suppliant ’ s god in my case , for I will follow you , both because I must and because it is my wish to die ; for if I were unwilling , a coward would I show myself , a woman faint of heart . Why should I prolong my days ? I whose father was lord

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: Φρυγῶν ἁπάντων · τοῦτό μοι πρῶτον βίου ·
sections.translation: of all the Phrygians , my chiefest pride in life . Then I was nursed on fair hopes to be a bride for kings , the center of keen jealousy among suitors , to see whose home I would make my own ; and over each lady of Ida I was queen ;

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: γυναιξὶ παρθένοις τʼ ἀπόβλεπτος μέτα ,
sections.translation: ah me ! admired among maidens , equal to a goddess , save for death alone , but now I am a slave ! That name first makes me long for death , so strange it sounds ; and then perhaps my lot might give me

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: τύχοιμʼ ἄν , ὅστις ἀργύρου μʼ ὠνήσεται ,
sections.translation: to some savage master , one that would buy me for money — me the sister of Hector and many others — who would make me knead him bread within his halls , or sweep his house or set me working at the loom , leading a life of misery ;

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: λέχη δὲ τἀμὰ δοῦλος ὠνητός ποθεν
sections.translation: while some slave , bought I know not where , will taint my bed , once deemed worthy of royalty . No , never ! Here I close my eyes upon the light , free as yet , and dedicate myself to Hades . Lead me away , Odysseus , and do your worst ,

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: οὔτʼ ἐλπίδος γὰρ οὔτε του δόξης ὁρῶ
sections.translation: for I see nothing within my reach to make me hope or expect with any confidence that I am ever again to be happy . Mother , do not seek to hinder me by word or deed , but join in my wish for death before I meet with shameful treatment undeserved .

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: ὅστις γὰρ οὐκ εἴωθε γεύεσθαι κακῶν ,
sections.translation: For whoever is not used to taste of sorrows , though he bears it , yet it galls him when he puts his neck within the yoke ; far happier would he be dead than alive , for life bereft of honor is toil and trouble .

sections.label: Section 379
sections.original: δεινὸς χαρακτὴρ κἀπίσημος ἐν βροτοῖς
sections.translation: A wondrous mark , most clearly stamped , does noble birth

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: ἐσθλῶν γενέσθαι , κἀπὶ μεῖζον ἔρχεται
sections.translation: imprint on men , and the name goes still further where it is deserved .

sections.label: Section 382
sections.original: καλῶς μὲν εἶπας , θύγατερ , ἀλλὰ τῷ καλῷ
sections.translation: A noble speech , my daughter ! but there is sorrow linked with its noble sentiments . Odysseus , if you must please the son of Peleus , and avoid reproach ,

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: ὑμᾶς , Ὀδυσσεῦ , τήνδε μὲν μὴ κτείνετε ,
sections.translation: do not slay this maid , but lead me to Achilles ’ pyre and torture me unsparingly ; it was I that bore Paris , whose fatal shaft laid low the son of Thetis .

sections.label: Section 389
sections.original: οὐ σʼ , ὦ γεραιά , κατθανεῖν Ἀχιλλέως
sections.translation: It is not your death , my lady , that Achilles ’ ghost

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: φάντασμʼ Ἀχαιούς , ἀλλὰ τήνδʼ , ᾐτήσατο .
sections.translation: has demanded of the Achaeans , but hers .

sections.label: Section 391
sections.original: ὑμεῖς δέ μʼ ἀλλὰ θυγατρὶ συμφονεύσατε ,
sections.translation: At least then slaughter me with my child ; so shall there be a double drink of blood for the earth and the dead that claims this sacrifice .

sections.label: Section 394
sections.original: ἅλις κόρης σῆς θάνατος , οὐ προσοιστέος
sections.translation: The maiden ’ s death suffices ; no need to add

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: ἄλλος πρὸς ἄλλῳ · μηδὲ τόνδʼ ὠφείλομεν .
sections.translation: a second to the first ; would we did not need even this !

sections.label: Section 396
sections.original: πολλή γʼ ἀνάγκη θυγατρὶ συνθανεῖν ἐμέ .
sections.translation: Die with my daughter I must and will .

sections.label: Section 397
sections.original: πῶς ; οὐ γὰρ οἶδα δεσπότας κεκτημένος .
sections.translation: How so ? I did not know I had a master .

sections.label: Section 398
sections.original: ὁποῖα κισσὸς δρυός , ὅπως τῆσδʼ ἕξομαι .
sections.translation: I will cling to her like ivy to an oak .

sections.label: Section 399
sections.original: οὔκ , ἤν γε πείθῃ τοῖσι σοῦ σοφωτέροις .
sections.translation: Not if you will listen to those who are wiser than you .
